today_summary: GCM26 spot $4,151.8 with max pain $4,795 sitting $643 (+15.5%) above — too distant to exert real pull at just 6 DTE. Chain is call-heavy (P/C OI ratio 0.53: 138,922 call OI vs 74,279 put OI), and ATM IV of 18.19% puts the 1σ daily move at ±$47.6 ($4,104–$4,199). Local dealer gamma is positive across $4,100–$4,350, with the largest concentration at $4,300 (net GEX +28.3), favoring range-bound pinning over trend continuation near spot.
five_day_thesis: Spot ripped $159.6 (+4.0%) over the last 5 sessions to $4,151.8 while max pain barely budged (+$5, pinned $4,790–$4,800 all week) — the max-pain gap narrowed from $798 to $643 but remains too wide at 6 DTE to matter tactically. The real story is the GEX regime flip: dealers ran short gamma for four straight sessions (07/17–07/22, window total -96.8 → -53.6 → -59.7 → -0.79) then flipped decisively positive today (+88.4), with the gamma flip strike drifting up from $3,195 to $3,325 as spot pushed through the call-heavy OI shelf. ATM IV compressed 2.74pp (20.93% → 18.19%) despite the rally — a vol crush consistent with a stabilizing dealer book rather than a squeeze. Put wall ($4,000, 4,091 ct) and call wall ($5,370, 8,008 ct) haven't moved once in 5 sessions, so the positioning skeleton is unchanged even as spot ran $160 through it. Net effect: today marks the first session in a week where dealer flows should dampen rather than amplify moves.
what_changed: GEX flipped from negative to positive today (window total -0.79 → +88.4) — first vol-dampening session in 5 days
Spot rallied $159.6 (+4.0%) over 5 sessions to $4,151.8, closing the max-pain gap from $798 to $643
ATM IV compressed 2.74pp (20.93% → 18.19%), the sharpest vol crush of the window
Gamma flip strike drifted higher, $3,195 → $3,325, tracking the spot rally
Put wall $4,000 (4,091 ct) and call wall $5,370 (8,008 ct) unchanged all 5 days — static positioning skeleton
Near-spot dealer gamma concentration now peaks at $4,300 (net GEX +28.3), the largest positive node in the $4,100–$4,350 shelf
prediction: Today's GEX flip to positive plus the rich positive-gamma shelf between $4,100–$4,350 (peak $4,300, net GEX +28.3) argues for compressed, pinning-type price action rather than trend continuation — the $643 max-pain gap is a 6-DTE distraction, not a near-term target. Bias is neutral-to-mildly-bullish while spot holds above the $4,100 1σ floor / gamma shelf; a break below the $4,000 put wall reopens the prior week's short-gamma amplification regime.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Positive GEX regime (flipped today from negative) favors range-bound pinning over trend continuation; max pain $4,795 is 15.5% away and too distant at 6 DTE to matter.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy dips into the $4,150 gamma node (net GEX +8.4, 3,081 call OI / 2,445 put OI) with the 1σ floor at $4,104 as the outer guide.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Sustained trade above → path to the $4,300 gamma wall (largest nearby positive net GEX, +28.3). Rejection here caps the session inside the $4,100–$4,200 range.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: Below the 1σ floor ($4,104) and the $4,100 OI node — loses the positive-gamma pin and reopens last week's short-gamma amplification regime.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 = largest nearby positive-gamma concentration (net GEX +28.3), take 50% off. T2 = $4,400 call wall (4,613 ct, heaviest call OI in the near window), exit remainder.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: GEX flipped positive today after 4 sessions negative — expect a compressed ±$47.6 (1σ) daily range, not last week's wider whipsaws.
